title: tina macrocarpa
intro: Tina macrocarpa, also known as the 'Red Stinkwood' or 'Tina', is a species of tree in the family Meliaceae. The tree is native to tropical Africa, and its bark, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial effects. The health benefits of Tina macrocarpa include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural remedy for digestive issues and wounds. Herbal preparations of Tina macrocarpa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
